What to do

  • You need to investigate an aspect of New Zealand music.
  • You will create a presentation to share your findings.
  • You are responsible for choosing your own research focus.

Key requirements

  • Research and analyze an aspect of NZ music.
  • Present your findings clearly and engagingly.
  • Show a deep understanding by making connections and evaluating significance.
  • Use credible sources and reference them correctly.
  • Explain how your topic adds to the diversity of the NZ music scene.
  • Provide broad perspectives, including your own interpretation.
  • Develop and answer a strong research question to guide your investigation.

Tips

  • Work with a partner to refine your topic into a strong research question.
  • Ensure your research question is focused, analytical, and allows for multiple perspectives.
  • Submit your final research question for teacher approval.
  • Use the scaffold to outline your presentation structure.
  • Present your outline to the class for feedback.

Dates & deadlines

  • None shown
